compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: DESIGN REFINEMENT (Joe, editability + de-risking): the batch step's group is a STATIC AUTHORED list of CRITERIA, each backed by a PROMPT-LIBRARY entry — the 'criteria library' mirrors the prompt library (1:1) so the same artifact wires into the eval seam (per-criterion eval = per-prompt eval) and the editor (add/remove/edit a criterion = its prompt). NOT a runtime-computed items list (which the visual editor cannot render/modify). Shape: batch: { prompt: <finder prompt-lib id>, criteria: [ {prompt: <criterion prompt-lib id>, when?: <overlay predicate>, with?: <inputs>} ], usd_budget?, model_ladder? }. The runner resolves each entry's when (reusing A1's overlay_triggers + predicate machinery), includes the truthy, packs the included criteria into budgeted batches under the shared finder, journals the plan (opaque to the interpreter). Reference runner (epic A): minimal-but-real — naive packing + per-batch finder call via AgentStepRunner + split-on-context-limit fallback; cost-escalation/shed are seam-supported but stubbed (epic B's production runner extracts sizing.py). This shape is editable (each criterion is a discrete authored node), mirrors the prompt library (eval+editing), and proves B's criteria-library wiring.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCOPE (Joe): build the full v3 path as part of A2 — ship workflow.v3.schema.json (v2 + the batch construct), the v2->v3 read-time up-conversion shim (batch is additive => effectively identity), and schema.py wiring (schema_version '3' -> v3 via the existing migrate shim), THEN the batch construct (step_kind/CONTROL_KINDS, interpreter _run_batch, runner seam + reference runner, journaled plan). Honors the immutable-versioned-schema discipline; v1->v2 up-conversion infra already exists to mirror.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Landed (commit d2a8a5c6). Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 10 advisory — param shapes/reference-runner/journaling all made concrete; the editability + criteria-library-mirrors-prompt-library refinement applied). Built the FULL v3 path per the immutable-schema discipline: workflow.v3.schema.json (= v2 + batch; v2 untouched), the v2->v3 pure-bump shim, schema.py wiring. The batch step uses the editable, prompt-library-backed criteria shape {prompt, when?, with?}; the BatchRunner seam + reference DefaultBatchRunner (split-on-context-limit real; cost/shed honestly stubbed -> epic B); the interpreter resolves each when (reusing if: machinery), delegates, and stores the OPAQUE batch_plan wholesale — a garbage-plan test proves it never branches on internals. Code-review subagent (general-purpose): clean — verified v2 untouched, the oneOf rejects mixes, the interpreter never inspects the plan, the split is bounded (depth<8, 1-criterion can't split), stubs honest; fixed the one minor finding (added batch to the type enum). Burr-tripwire green; full suite 3246 passed/13 skipped (run ALONE, HEAD stable); make lint clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS**

Verified all five acceptance criteria against the implementation (commit d2a8a5c6 per the landed comment). Every requirement is met.

---

**AC1 — `batch` step parses + schema-validates; bad params are validation errors.**

- `src/rebar/schemas/workflow.v3.schema.json` defines the `batch` $def with `required: [prompt, criteria]`, optional `usd_budget` (number, exclusiveMinimum: 0) and `model_ladder` (array of strings); the `criterion` $def has `required: [prompt]` + `additionalProperties: false`. The `step` oneOf includes a `batch` arm that forbids mixing with `uses`/`prompt`/`branch`/`loop`/`map`.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/schema.py` wires `CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = "3"`, `SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSIONS = ("1", "2", "3")`, and `step_kind` classifies `"batch"` as a distinct kind (not in CONTROL_KINDS, which stays `("branch", "loop", "map")` per design).
- `tests/unit/workflow/test_batch_step.py` covers: valid batch validates (→ `[]` errors), missing `criteria` is an error, bad `usd_budget` type is an error, mixing `uses`+`batch` is an error, unknown criterion key is an error. **MET.**

**AC2 — Batch-runner SEAM exists with reference default runner; journals batch-plan + fallback transitions as opaque recorded events.**

- `executor.py` lines 346–450 define `BatchRunRequest`, `BatchRunResult`, the `BatchRunner` seam (protocol), and `DefaultBatchRunner` (reference runner).
- `DefaultBatchRunner.run` journals a `batch_plan` dict with `finder`, `max_batch_size`, `usd_budget`, `model_ladder`, `enforced=False` (honest stub for cost/shed → epic B), `batches` (each entry `{criteria, model, outcome: "ran"|"split"}`), and `shed`. The split-on-context-limit path is real (lines 441–447, bounded at depth < 8).
- `test_batch_journals_a_plan`, `test_runner_splits_a_batch_on_context_limit` verify the journaled plan and the split fallback. **MET.**

**AC3 — Interpreter stores journaled plan as step output; NEVER branches on internals (test asserts this); replay reads the recorded plan.**

- `interpreter.py::_run_batch` (lines 348–392) copies `result.outputs` (including the opaque `batch_plan`) wholesale via `dict(result.outputs)` and calls `_commit_state` — no inspection of `batch_plan` internals occurs anywhere in `_run_batch` or `_execute_frame`.
- Replay idempotency: `_run_batch` lines 356–361 call `rc.rec.completed_step(rc.run_id, frame_key)` and return the recorded outputs directly when a succeeded marker exists, never re-running the runner.
- `test_interpreter_does_not_branch_on_plan_internals` passes a `_GarbagePlanRunner` emitting an intentionally malformed plan and asserts the step still succeeds with the plan stored verbatim. **MET.**

**AC4 — Burr-tripwire stays green (no asyncio/threading/concurrent leaks outside the confined fan-out path).**

- `test_executor_tripwire.py` concatenates `executor.py` + `interpreter.py` source and AST-scans for banned modules (`asyncio`, `concurrent`, `threading`, `multiprocessing`, `tenacity`, `backoff`, `retrying`, `retry`).
- `interpreter.py` imports only stdlib (`graphlib`, `os`, `re`, `collections.abc`, `contextlib`, `dataclasses`, `typing`) and local modules — none of the banned modules. The test also separately asserts `map_fanout.py` IS the only concurrency relaxation and that neither `executor.py` nor `interpreter.py` imports concurrency libs. **MET.**

**AC5 — make lint + workflow test suite pass.**

- The full `tests/unit/workflow/` suite includes `test_batch_step.py` (12 tests covering schema, migration, inclusion-by-when, journaling, split, and the never-branch assertion) and updated `test_migrate.py` (v2→v3 golden roundtrip, v1 chains to v3, both shims registered). Code is syntactically and structurally correct; the landed comment reports 3246 passed / 13 skipped. **MET.**

**Scope items** (from the SCOPE comment): `workflow.v3.schema.json` shipped (v2 untouched — confirmed no "batch" in `workflow.v2.schema.json`); v2→v3 pure-bump shim registered (`migrate.py::_v2_to_v3`, `_SHIMS = {"1": _v1_to_v2, "2": _v2_to_v3}`); `schemas/__init__.py` exports `WORKFLOW_V3 = "workflow.v3"`; `run_workflow` accepts and defaults `batch_runner: BatchRunner | None`; `_RunCtx` carries `batch_runner`. All scope items present.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

A review pipeline runs N criteria-prompts in budgeted BATCHES (one LLM call per batch), with runtime
fallback (split -> one-per-call -> escalate model) on a context-limit. No surveyed engine makes
adaptive cost-budgeted batching first-class — it is app code everywhere — and encoding it in the IR
would break replay determinism (batch composition depends on a runtime token estimate + which call
hit a context limit). So the IR gets a THIN step that delegates the adaptive work to a runner.

## What

Add a `batch` step kind whose IR surface is purely declarative parameters: `group` (the prompt set),
`usd_budget`, `model_ladder`. A batch-runner SEAM (protocol) performs the adaptive packing /
token-estimation / context-limit fallback / model-escalation / shed-to-INDETERMINATE / fingerprint-
checkpoint, and JOURNALS its batch-plan + fallback transitions as OPAQUE events. The interpreter
STORES the journaled plan as an output but NEVER branches on its internals (thin-interpreter / Burr-
tripwire preserved). Ship a reference/default batch-runner; epic B supplies the production runner
(extracted from the gate's sizing.py) behind this seam.

## Scope

- src/rebar/llm/workflow/schema.py + JSON Schema: the `batch` step kind + its params + validation.
- A batch-runner protocol/seam (alongside AgentStepRunner in executor.py) + a reference default runner.
- The journaled batch-plan / fallback event vocabulary (recorded decisions, not recomputed).
- src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py: invoke the batch-runner, store the opaque plan as the step
  output; the interpreter must not interpret batch internals.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `batch` step parses + schema-validates with `group`/`usd_budget`/`model_ladder`; bad params
      are validation errors.
- [ ] A batch-runner SEAM exists with a reference default runner; the runner journals its batch-plan
      + fallback transitions as opaque recorded events.
- [ ] The interpreter stores the journaled plan as the step output and NEVER branches on its
      internals (a test asserts this); replay reads the recorded plan rather than recomputing it.
- [ ] The Burr-tripwire stays green (no asyncio/threading/concurrent leaks outside the confined
      fan-out path).
- [ ] make lint + the workflow test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: v3 = v2 + the batch construct (v2 untouched, immutability discipline)
BatchRunner seam + DefaultBatchRunner + run_workflow batch_runner param
_run_batch: resolve when, delegate, store opaque plan, never branch

compiled_state.title: Batch step: a thin delegating step kind + batch-runner seam + reference runner (journaled batch-plan)
